Tomato___Late_blight.remedy: Apply a protectant fungicide containing chlorothalonil or mancozeb and remove heavily infected leaves.
Tomato___Late_blight.dosage: Chlorothalonil 2 g per liter water or mancozeb 2.5 g per liter, spray every 7 to 10 days.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation, improve airflow by pruning, and rotate away from solanaceous crops for 2 seasons.

Tomato___Early_blight.remedy: Use azoxystrobin or copper-based fungicide and remove lower infected foliage.
Tomato___Early_blight.dosage: Azoxystrobin 1 ml per liter or copper oxychloride 2.5 g per liter, repeat at 10 day interval.
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Use mulching to reduce soil splash, maintain balanced potassium, and practice crop rotation.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.remedy: Spray copper hydroxide or chlorothalonil and reduce greenhouse humidity.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.dosage: Copper hydroxide 2 g per liter, apply at first symptoms and repeat after 7 days.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention: Ensure spacing and ventilation, irrigate at root zone only, and sanitize crop residues.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.remedy: Apply mancozeb or chlorothalonil and remove severely affected leaves.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.dosage: Mancozeb 2.5 g per liter, 2 to 3 sprays at 7 day interval depending on disease pressure.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: Stake plants, avoid wet foliage, and remove volunteer tomato plants after harvest.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.remedy: Spray neem oil or abamectin and target leaf undersides thoroughly.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.dosage: Neem oil 3 ml per liter or abamectin 0.5 ml per liter, repeat after 5 to 7 days.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention: Reduce dust, conserve natural predators, and avoid repeated broad-spectrum insecticides.

Tomato___Target_Spot.remedy: Use fungicides containing difenoconazole or mancozeb with sanitation pruning.
Tomato___Target_Spot.dosage: Difenoconazole 0.5 ml per liter or mancozeb 2.5 g per liter, spray every 10 days.
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: Maintain field hygiene, avoid water stress, and ensure proper plant spacing.

Tomato___YellowLeaf_Curl_Virus.remedy: Control whitefly vectors using yellow traps and selective insecticide programs.
Tomato___YellowLeaf_Curl_Virus.dosage: Imidacloprid 0.3 ml per liter at early stage, follow local resistance management guidance.
Tomato___YellowLeaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: Use virus-free seedlings, rogue infected plants early, and install insect-proof nursery nets.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.remedy: Apply copper bactericide with mancozeb tank mix and avoid handling wet plants.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.dosage: Copper oxychloride 2 g plus mancozeb 2 g per liter, repeat every 7 days in humid weather.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use certified seed, sanitize tools, and rotate with non-host crops for at least 1 year.

Tomato___healthy.remedy: No treatment required. Continue integrated nutrient and pest management.
Tomato___healthy.prevention: Scout weekly, maintain balanced fertilization, and irrigate uniformly to avoid stress.

Potato___Late_blight.remedy: Spray systemic plus contact fungicide and destroy infected haulms if severe.
Potato___Late_blight.dosage: Cymoxanil plus mancozeb 2 g per liter, repeat every 7 days during cool humid periods.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Use resistant varieties, avoid evening irrigation, and hill soil to protect tubers.

Potato___Early_blight.remedy: Apply chlorothalonil or azoxystrobin and remove infected lower foliage.
Potato___Early_blight.dosage: Chlorothalonil 2 g per liter, spray at symptom onset and repeat after 10 days.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Ensure adequate potassium, rotate fields, and prevent prolonged leaf wetness.

Potato___healthy.remedy: No treatment required. Continue preventive scouting and nutrition.
Potato___healthy.prevention: Use clean seed tubers, maintain drainage, and monitor canopy health regularly.

Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.remedy: Use copper-based bactericide and remove heavily infected leaves and fruits.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.dosage: Copper hydroxide 2 g per liter at 7 to 10 day interval.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use pathogen-free seed, avoid overhead irrigation, and disinfect tools and trays.

Pepper__bell___healthy.remedy: No treatment needed. Maintain routine crop monitoring.
Pepper__bell___healthy.prevention: Rotate crops and maintain field sanitation to reduce inoculum build-up.

Corn___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_sp.remedy: Apply triazole or strobilurin fungicide when lesions progress to upper canopy.
Corn___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_sp.dosage: Propiconazole 1 ml per liter or equivalent product as label recommendation.
Corn___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_sp.prevention: Use tolerant hybrids, manage residue, and rotate with non-host crops.

Corn___Common_rust.remedy: Spray fungicide if rust appears before tasseling and disease is spreading.
Corn___Common_rust.dosage: Tebuconazole 1 ml per liter, one to two sprays based on scouting.
Corn___Common_rust.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids and avoid excessive late nitrogen application.

Corn___Northern_Leaf_Blight.remedy: Apply fungicide at early lesion stage and monitor field progression.
Corn___Northern_Leaf_Blight.dosage: Azoxystrobin 1 ml per liter, repeat in 10 to 14 days if needed.
Corn___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention: Use resistant cultivars, rotate crops, and bury infected residue where feasible.

Corn___healthy.remedy: No treatment needed. Continue best agronomy practices.
Corn___healthy.prevention: Scout at regular intervals and maintain balanced fertilization and irrigation.
